chapter_goal: Show why the Kahler identities force de Rham cohomology to split by type and support Lefschetz sl2 structure.
checks: toy commutator H=[Lambda,L] has expected weights
intersection form signature matches a surface example
lab: Build Lefschetz strings for a toy surface and identify primitive classes by the failed next raise.
library_routes: Matplotlib for operator board and Lefschetz strings
SymPy for commutator checks in a toy exterior algebra
pitfalls: using the Hodge decomposition without compact Kahler hypotheses
forgetting primitive means killed after enough Lefschetz raising
mixing the Hodge index form with a positive definite metric
proof_moves: use L and Lambda to build weight strings
separate primitive pieces before applying powers of the Kahler class
visual_focus: An operator commutator board makes the identities and the Lefschetz string decomposition inspectable.
